title: King Activity Wins
setup: Pawn on **e3**, your king on **e4**, the black king sitting on f6 trying to keep watch over the pawn's promotion square.
hint: Don't go straight forward — **gain ground** by stepping to a square the enemy king cannot reach.
explanation:
## Key Principle: Gaining Squares with the King

The black king is doing its job: it covers e5, e6 and e7, slowing your pawn. To make progress, your king must reach the 6th rank without trading itself off. The way to do that is to step **diagonally forward** to a square the enemy king cannot dispute.

## The Plan

1. **{{Kd5}}** — White advances toward the queenside, gaining a rank without ever standing on a square Black can immediately challenge. Black cannot reply Kd5 (occupied), Ke5 (attacked by your king), or Kf5 (attacked).
2. Black's best try is **...Ke7**, hurrying back to defend. White answers with **Kc6** or **Ke5**, continuing to push the enemy king while keeping the pawn safe.
3. When the white king reaches the 6th rank, the pawn marches: **e4, e5, e6** — and the king clears the queening square.

## What Goes Wrong If You Push the Pawn

If White plays 1.e4? right away, the king on f6 is now well-placed: 1...Ke6! and Black has secured the **square in front of the pawn**. From there it is a fortress and a draw. The lesson: **before pushing, win the squares your pawn will stand on**.
